get.description: Returns the available variables for a given email type.
Retrieves a realm by its name. This endpoint returns the details of the specified realm.

put.description: Updates the name of an existing realm. This endpoint allows you to change the name of a realm while keeping its associated data intact.

delete.summary: Delete a realm by name
delete.description: Deletes a realm by its name. This action is irreversible and will remove all associated data.

get.description: Get the login settings for a specific realm.

put.summary: Update settings for a realm by name
put.description: Updates the settings for a specified realm. This endpoint allows modification of various realm settings.

get.description: Retrieves the OpenID Connect configuration for a specific realm. This endpoint provides metadata about the OpenID Connect provider, including endpoints for authorization, token issuance, introspection, user information, and JWKs.

post.summary: Handle SSO callback from identity provider
post.description: Processes the IdP callback, exchanges code for tokens, and redirects to client

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Error code from IdP (if authentication failed)

302.description: Redirect to client with authorization code

400.description: Bad request - invalid state or expired session

get.summary: Initiate SSO login via identity provider
get.description: Redirects the user to the external identity provider for authentication

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: Redirect URI to return to after authentication

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Client's state parameter for CSRF protection

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Existing auth session ID (if initiated from login page)

302.description: Redirect to identity provider authorization URL

get.summary: Get client scopes in a realm
get.description: Retrieves all client scopes associated with a specific realm.

post.description: Creates a new client scope within the specified realm.

get.description: Retrieves a client scope from the specified realm by its ID.

delete.summary: Delete a client scope
delete.description: Deletes a client scope from the specified realm. This action is irreversible.

patch.description: Updates an existing client scope in the specified realm.

post.description: Creates a new protocol mapper for the specified client scope.

delete.summary: Delete a protocol mapper
delete.description: Deletes a protocol mapper from the specified client scope.

patch.description: Updates an existing protocol mapper for the specified client scope.

get.description: Retrieves all clients associated with a specific realm.

post.summary: Create a new client in a realm
post.description: Creates a new client within the specified realm. This endpoint allows you to register a new client application that can interact with the realm's resources.

get.description: Retrieves a client from the specified realm by its ID.

delete.summary: Delete a client
delete.description: Deletes a client from the specified realm. This action is irreversible and will remove all associated data.

patch.description: Updates an existing client in the specified realm. This endpoint allows you to modify client details such as name, client ID, and enabled status.

get.summary: Get client scopes assigned to a client
get.description: Returns all client scopes (default and optional) assigned to the specified client.

put.summary: Assign a default client scope to a client
put.description: Assigns a client scope as a default scope to the specified client. Default scopes are always included in tokens.

delete.summary: Remove a default client scope from a client
delete.description: Removes a client scope from the default scopes of the specified client.

put.summary: Assign an optional client scope to a client
put.description: Assigns a client scope as an optional scope to the specified client. Optional scopes are included only when explicitly requested.

delete.summary: Remove an optional client scope from a client
delete.description: Removes a client scope from the optional scopes of the specified client.

get.summary: Get post-logout redirect URIs for a client
get.description: Retrieves all post-logout redirect URIs associated with a client in a specific realm.

post.summary: Create a new post-logout redirect URI for a client
post.description: Creates a new post-logout redirect URI for the specified client.

201.description: Post-logout redirect URI created successfully for the client

put.summary: Update a post-logout redirect URI for a client
put.description: Updates an existing post-logout redirect URI for a client in a specific realm.

delete.summary: Delete a post-logout redirect URI for a client
delete.description: Deletes a specific post-logout redirect URI for a client in a realm.

get.summary: Get redirect URIs for a client
get.description: Retrieves all redirect URIs associated with a client in a specific realm. This endpoint is useful for OAuth2 or OpenID Connect flows where clients need to know their registered redirect URIs.

post.summary: Create a new redirect URI for a client
post.description: Creates a new redirect URI for the specified client. This endpoint allows you to add a redirect URI that the client can use for OAuth2 or OpenID Connect flows.

201.description: Redirect URI created successfully for the client

put.summary: Update a redirect URI for a client
put.description: Updates an existing redirect URI for a client in a specific realm. This endpoint allows you to modify the enabled status of a redirect URI.

delete.summary: Delete a redirect URI for a client
delete.description: Deletes a specific redirect URI for a client in a realm. This action is irreversible and will remove the redirect URI from the client's configuration.

get.description: Retrieves all roles associated with a client in a specific realm.

post.description: Creates a new role for a specific client within a realm. This endpoint allows you to define roles that can be assigned to users or groups in the context of a client application.

get.description: Retrieves all email templates for the specified realm.

post.description: Creates a new email template for the specified realm.

get.description: Retrieves a single email template by ID.

delete.summary: Delete email template

patch.description: Activates an email template, deactivating any other active template of the same type in this realm.

get.summary: List federation providers in a realm
Get a federation provider details by ID

delete.summary: Delete a federation provider

post.description: Triggers synchronization of users from the external federation provider (LDAP, Kerberos, etc.) to the local IAM database. This performs a comprehensive diff and reconciliation.
Tests the connection to the external federation provider (LDAP, Kerberos, etc.) to verify configuration and connectivity

get.summary: List all identity providers in a realm
get.description: Retrieves all identity providers configured for the specified realm. Optionally returns a brief representation with fewer fields.

post.summary: Create a new identity provider in a realm
post.description: Creates a new identity provider configuration for the specified realm. The identity provider will be used for social login (Google, GitHub, etc.) or OIDC/SAML federation.

400.description: Invalid configuration or missing required fields

get.summary: Get an identity provider by alias
get.description: Retrieves the details of a specific identity provider by its alias. Sensitive configuration values (like client secrets) are redacted in the response.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: The unique alias of the identity provider

404.description: Identity provider or realm not found

put.description: Updates an existing identity provider configuration. Only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. The alias cannot be changed after creation.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: The unique alias of the identity provider to update

404.description: Identity provider or realm not found

delete.summary: Delete an identity provider
delete.description: Deletes an identity provider from the realm. This action is irreversible. Users who have linked accounts with this identity provider will no longer be able to use it for authentication.

parameters.name: alias
parameters.description: The unique alias of the identity provider to delete

404.description: Identity provider or realm not found

409.description: Identity provider is still in use

post.summary: Authenticate a user in a realm
Burn a recovery code to authenticate
post.description: Using a recovery code allows a user to bypass a MFA challenge
Challenges the user to provide a One-Time Password (OTP) for authentication. This is typically used in multi-factor authentication scenarios.
Sends a password reset email to the user if the email exists in the realm. Always returns 204 to prevent email enumeration.

200.description: Request processed (email sent if user exists)

post.description: Generates recovery codes that allows the user to bypass a MFA challenge
Complete passkey authentication by submitting the browser's assertion response. On success, returns a login URL with an authorization code.

Initiates a passkey authentication flow. If username is provided, returns a challenge scoped to that user's passkeys. If omitted, returns a discoverable challenge allowing the browser to propose available passkeys.
Completes the password reset flow by verifying the token and setting a new password. Returns authentication tokens to log the user in directly.

200.description: Password reset successfully, returns auth tokens

post.summary: Send magic link for passwordless authentication
post.description: Sends a magic link to the user's email for passwordless authentication. The link contains a unique token that can be used to verify the user's identity.

get.description: Sets up a One-Time Password (OTP) for user authentication. This is typically used in multi-factor authentication scenarios.

get.summary: Verify magic link and complete authentication
get.description: Verifies the magic link token and completes the authentication flow. Returns authentication status and redirect URL with authorization code.

parameters.name: token_id
parameters.description: The unique token identifier from the magic link

parameters.name: magic_token
parameters.description: The secret verification token from the magic link

post.description: Verifies the One-Time Password (OTP) provided by the user. This is typically used in multi-factor authentication scenarios.
Checks if a password reset token exists and has not expired, without consuming it.

Attempt authentication using a WebAuthnAssertionResponse payload for webauthn authentication. See https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dictdef-authenticationresponsejson and https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#authenticatorassertionresponse

post.summary: Validate and save a webauthn public key
post.description: Saving a webauthn public key to use it for authentication attempts or MFA later.

Provides a full PublicKeyCredentialCreationOption payload for WebAuthn credential creation/authentication. The payload contains the challenge to resolve in B64Url form as described in the specs. The content is described here: https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dictdef-publickeycredentialcreationoptions.

200.description: WebAuthn public key creation options generated successfully

post.description: Provides a full PublicKeyCredentialRequestOption payload for webauthn authentication. See https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dictdef-publickeycredentialrequestoptions and https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dictdef-publickeycredentialrequestoptionsjson

200.description: WebAuthn public key request options generated successfully

delete.summary: Delete an organization

put.summary: Create or update an organization attribute

delete.summary: Delete an organization attribute

get.summary: Get password policy for a realm
get.description: Retrieves the password policy for the specified realm.

put.summary: Update password policy for a realm
put.description: Updates the password policy for the specified realm.

get.description: Initiates the authentication process for a user in a specific realm.

302.description: Redirects to the login page with session cookie set

get.summary: Get JWK keys for a realm
get.description: Retrieves the JSON Web Key (JWK) keys for a specific realm, used for verifying JWT tokens.
Retrieves the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) for a specific realm, used by resource servers to validate JWT signatures.
Ends the user's OP session. Supports id_token_hint, post_logout_redirect_uri, state, and client_id.

post.description: Ends the user's OP session. Supports id_token_hint, post_logout_redirect_uri, state, and client_id.
Register a new user in the specified realm

403.description: User registration is disabled for this realm

post.description: OAuth2 token revocation endpoint (RFC 7009). Revokes access or refresh tokens for the requesting client.
Exchanges a token for a JWT token. This endpoint allows clients to exchange various types of tokens (like authorization codes, refresh tokens, etc.) for a JWT token.

OAuth2/OIDC Token Introspection (RFC 7662). Only confidential clients may call this endpoint using client_secret_basic or client_secret_post. Authorization requires the caller's service account to have the role `introspect` (treated as the `introspect` scope).

get.description: Retrieves the user information for the authenticated user in a specific realm.

get.summary: Get all roles for a realm

post.description: Creates a new realm-scoped role in the specified realm.

get.summary: Get a role by ID in a realm

put.summary: Update a role in a realm

delete.summary: Delete a role in a realm

patch.summary: Update a role in a realm

get.summary: Get SMTP configuration for a realm
get.description: Retrieves the SMTP configuration for the specified realm. The password field is omitted from the response.

put.summary: Create or update SMTP configuration for a realm
put.description: Creates or updates the SMTP configuration for the specified realm.

delete.summary: Delete SMTP configuration for a realm
delete.description: Deletes the SMTP configuration for the specified realm.

get.summary: Get all users in a realm
get.description: Retrieves all users associated with a specific realm.

post.summary: Create a new user in a realm

delete.summary: Bulk delete users in a realm
delete.description: Deletes multiple users in a realm by their IDs. This action is irreversible and will remove all associated data.

get.summary: Get a user by ID in a realm
get.description: Retrieves a user by their ID in a specific realm. This endpoint returns detailed information about the user.

put.summary: Update a user in a realm
put.description: Updates an existing user in a specific realm. The user must exist and the request must include the necessary fields to update.

delete.summary: Delete a user in a realm
delete.description: Deletes a user in a realm. This action is irreversible and will remove all associated data.

get.summary: Get user credentials in a realm
get.description: Retrieves all credentials associated with a user in a specific realm.

delete.summary: Delete a user credential in a realm
delete.description: Deletes a specific credential for a user in a realm. This action is irreversible and will remove all associated data.

get.summary: Get user permissions by ID in a realm
get.description: Retrieves the permissions assigned to a user by their ID in a specific realm.

put.description: Resets the password for a user in a specific realm.

get.summary: Get all roles for a specific user
get.description: Retrieves all roles associated with a user in a specific realm.

post.summary: Assign a role to a user in a realm
post.description: Assigns a specified role to a user within a given realm. This endpoint is used to manage user roles in the system.

delete.summary: Unassign a role from a user in a realm
delete.description: Unassigns a specific role from a user in a realm. This action is irreversible and will remove the user's access to the role's permissions.

get.description: Retrieves a list of all webhooks available in the system related to the current realm.

post.description: Creates a new webhook in the system related to the current realm.

get.description: Retrieves one webhook in the system related to the current realm.

put.description: Updates a webhook in the system related to the current realm.

delete.summary: Delete webhook
delete.description: Deletes a webhook in the system related to the current realm.

Organization.description:
Organization domain entity

`name` is the primary human-readable label shown in admin or end-user surfaces.
`alias` is the stable URL-safe identifier used for lookups and routing within a realm.
The first organizations release does not persist a separate `display_name`, so callers
should rely on `name` as the display label.

PasskeyPublicKeyCredential.description: PublicKeyCredential assertion response for passkey authentication. See https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dictdef-publickeycredentialjson
